definition:
γέμω (used only in present and impf.), 
 [in LXX for מלא, נשׂא ;] 
to be full; 
  1. properly, of a ship (Xen.). 
   2. Generally (Plat., al.), 
  (a) with genitive of thing(s): Mat.23:27, Luk.11:39, Rom.3:14" (LXX), Rev.4:6, 8 5:8 15:7 17:3 (Rec.) 4 21:9 
   (b) before ἐκ: Mat.23:25; 
   (with) with accusative of thing(s) (called a solecism in WM, 251; Bl., §36, 4), as in later Gk. from Byz. to Mod. times (Jannaris, Gr., 1319): Rev.17:3.
 † (AS)
